table_description: CRITICAL junction table. This is the ONLY way to connect agent_logins to policies, customers, vehicles, coverages, claims, and billing. Every agent-related query MUST go through this table.

table_columns.name: customer_ref
table_columns.description: Customer on the policy. JOIN to customers.system_id.

sample_queries.question: How many active policies does acmegroup have?
sample_queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS active_policy_count FROM agent_logins al JOIN provider_policy_access ppa ON al.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref JOIN policies p ON ppa.policy_system_id = p.system_id WHERE al.login_id = 'acmegroup' AND p.status = 'Active'

sample_queries.question: Which customers belong to agent acmea1?
sample_queries.sql: SELECT DISTINCT ppa.customer_ref, c.index_name FROM agent_logins al JOIN provider_policy_access ppa ON al.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref LEFT JOIN customers c ON ppa.customer_ref = c.system_id WHERE al.login_id = 'acmea1'

sample_queries.question: What is the total premium for all active policies under Acme Group?
sample_queries.sql: SELECT SUM(p.full_term_amt) AS total_premium FROM agent_logins al JOIN provider_policy_access ppa ON al.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref JOIN policies p ON ppa.policy_system_id = p.system_id WHERE al.login_id = 'acmegroup' AND p.status = 'Active'

sample_queries.question: How many vehicles are insured under acmea1?
sample_queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT v.id) AS vehicle_count FROM agent_logins al JOIN provider_policy_access ppa ON al.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref JOIN vehicles v ON v.policy_id = ppa.policy_system_id WHERE al.login_id = 'acmea1'

sample_queries.question: Which customer under acmea1 has the most vehicles?
sample_queries.sql: SELECT c.index_name, COUNT(DISTINCT v.id) AS vehicle_count FROM agent_logins al JOIN provider_policy_access ppa ON al.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref JOIN vehicles v ON v.policy_id = ppa.policy_system_id JOIN customers c ON ppa.customer_ref = c.system_id WHERE al.login_id = 'acmea1' GROUP BY c.index_name ORDER BY vehicle_count DESC LIMIT 5

sample_queries.question: Show all claims for policies under acmeagpa
sample_queries.sql: SELECT cl.claim_number, cl.status, cl.loss_date, cl.description FROM agent_logins al JOIN provider_policy_access ppa ON al.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref JOIN claims cl ON cl.policy_ref = ppa.policy_system_id WHERE al.login_id = 'acmeagpa'

sample_queries.question: List each agent with their active policy count
sample_queries.sql: SELECT al.first_name, al.last_name, al.login_id, COUNT(DISTINCT ppa.policy_system_id) AS active_policies FROM agent_logins al JOIN provider_policy_access ppa ON al.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref JOIN policies p ON ppa.policy_system_id = p.system_id WHERE p.status = 'Active' GROUP BY al.login_id, al.first_name, al.last_name ORDER BY active_policies DESC

sample_queries.question: Show coverage details for policies under Acme Group
sample_queries.sql: SELECT cov.coverage_code, cov.description, SUM(cov.premium_amt) AS total_premium FROM agent_logins al JOIN provider_policy_access ppa ON al.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref JOIN vehicles v ON v.policy_id = ppa.policy_system_id JOIN coverages cov ON cov.vehicle_id = v.id WHERE al.login_id = 'acmegroup' GROUP BY cov.coverage_code, cov.description ORDER BY total_premium DESC

rules: CRITICAL: This table is the ONLY correct way to link agent_logins to ANY other data (policies, customers, vehicles, coverages, claims, billing).

The join chain is ALWAYS: agent_logins.provider_ref = ppa.provider_ref, THEN ppa.policy_system_id = policies.system_id (for policies), OR ppa.customer_ref = customers.system_id (for customers), OR vehicles.policy_id = ppa.policy_system_id (for vehicles).
WRONG: providers.index_name = 'acmegroup' then policies.provider_ref = providers.system_id (WILL RETURN 0 ROWS)
Agent hierarchy: acmegroup (11111) is the group, acmeagtx (22222) and acmeagpa (33333) are agencies, acmea1-4 (101-104) are individual agents. ALL use the same junction table pattern.
